# linked2pay - integration for Acumatica

> linked2pay is an Accounts Receivable customer-payment integration for Acumatica ERP. Integration version 2.0.0 is validated with Acumatica 2025 R2 build 25.201.0213. Accounts Payable and vendor payments are outside the current scope.

## Current Acumatica integration scope

- Unified customer Sync: create, update, or recreate a missing linked2pay contact
- Read-only customer Contact Profile ID
- Tokenized card and ACH wallet synchronization by Payment Profile ID
- Create, update, reactivate, and deactivate mirrored customer payment methods
- linked2pay Center bulk customer sync, cash-account assignment, and default-payment assignment
- Customer card and ACH payments, voids, and refunds
- Automatic credit-card fee calculation with recoverable CCFEE debit-memo accounting
- Configurable sandbox or production API Base URL
- Authenticated, idempotent webhooks with retryable unmatched events and configurable logging retention
- Duplicate-charge prevention, ambiguous-result reconciliation, and captured-but-unreleased recovery
- Level 3 to Level 2 to standard card-processing fallback when enabled and safe
- linked2pay Auto-Bill for all eligible due AR invoices
- Unattended recurring invoice generation followed by scheduled Auto-Bill
- Modern and Classic UI support for customized customer and payment workflows

## Not currently supported

- Outbound Accounts Payable or vendor payments
- Pulling new customers from linked2pay into Acumatica
- Invoice synchronization between Acumatica and linked2pay
- linked2pay invoice email links
- Automatic Acumatica accounting documents for chargebacks or ACH returns

## Audience

This site is written for Acumatica customers, executives, finance teams, developers, implementation consultants, and value-added resellers evaluating or deploying customer payment processing.

## Contact

- Product questions and demonstrations: sales@linked2pay.com
- Technical and implementation support: support@linked2pay.com

## Terminology

Use "linked2pay" in lowercase, "Acumatica" with initial capitalization, "ACH" in uppercase, and "linked2pay Auto-Bill" for the processing screen. "Payments" means customer Accounts Receivable collections unless a page explicitly says otherwise.
